examples.title: FCFS Scheduling Algorithm
examples.description: First Come First Serve algorithm implementation demonstrating waiting time calculation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FCFS_Scheduling
INPUT: Array of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Average waiting time and turnaround time

BEGIN
  Sort processes by arrival time
  current_time = 0
  total_waiting = 0
  total_turnaround = 0

  FOR each process P in sorted_list DO
    IF current_time < P.arrival_time THEN
      current_time = P.arrival_time
    END IF

    P.waiting_time = current_time - P.arrival_time
    total_waiting = total_waiting + P.waiting_time

    current_time = current_time + P.burst_time
    P.turnaround_time = current_time - P.arrival_time
    total_turnaround = total_turnaround + P.turnaround_time
  END FOR

  avg_waiting = total_waiting / number_of_processes
  avg_turnaround = total_turnaround / number_of_processes

  OUTPUT avg_waiting, avg_turnaround
END
examples.explanation: FCFS executes processes in order of arrival. Simple but can cause long waiting times for short processes that arrive after long ones. Demonstrates how waiting time accumulates based on queue position.

examples.title: SJF Non-Preemptive Scheduling
examples.description: Shortest Job First algorithm that minimizes average waiting time
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SJF_Scheduling
INPUT: Array of processes with arrival time and burst time
OUTPUT: Average waiting time and turnaround time

BEGIN
  ready_queue = []
  completed = 0
  current_time = 0
  total_waiting = 0
  total_turnaround = 0

  WHILE completed < number_of_processes DO
    // Add all arrived processes to ready queue
    FOR each process P DO
      IF P.arrival_time <= current_time AND NOT P.completed THEN
        Add P to ready_queue
      END IF
    END FOR

    // Sort by burst time (shortest first)
    Sort ready_queue by burst_time ASC

    IF ready_queue is not empty THEN
      selected = ready_queue[0]  // Shortest job
      Remove selected from ready_queue

      selected.waiting_time = current_time - selected.arrival_time
      total_waiting = total_waiting + selected.waiting_time

      current_time = current_time + selected.burst_time
      selected.turnaround_time = current_time - selected.arrival_time
      total_turnaround = total_turnaround + selected.turnaround_time

      selected.completed = TRUE
      completed = completed + 1
    ELSE
      current_time = current_time + 1  // Idle time
    END IF
  END WHILE

  OUTPUT total_waiting/n, total_turnaround/n
END
examples.explanation: SJF selects the process with smallest burst time from ready queue, minimizing average waiting time. However, can cause starvation for long processes if short ones keep arriving.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n²) with sorting at each step

examples.title: Round Robin Scheduling
examples.description: Time-sliced scheduling algorithm ensuring fairness through equal time quantum
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Round_Robin
INPUT: Processes, time_quantum
OUTPUT: Average waiting time and turnaround time

BEGIN
  ready_queue = queue()
  current_time = 0
  total_waiting = 0
  total_turnaround = 0

  // Initialize: add all arriving at time 0
  FOR each process P with arrival = 0 DO
    ready_queue.enqueue(P)
  END FOR

  WHILE ready_queue is not empty DO
    current_process = ready_queue.dequeue()

    // Record waiting time before execution
    current_process.waiting_time = current_time - current_process.arrival_time
    total_waiting = total_waiting + current_process.waiting_time

    // Execute for time_quantum or remaining burst
    execute_time = MIN(time_quantum, current_process.remaining_time)
    current_time = current_time + execute_time
    current_process.remaining_time = current_process.remaining_time - execute_time

    // Check if new processes arrived during execution
    FOR each process P DO
      IF P.arrival_time <= current_time AND NOT P.in_queue THEN
        ready_queue.enqueue(P)
        P.in_queue = TRUE
      END IF
    END FOR

    // If process not complete, re-queue
    IF current_process.remaining_time > 0 THEN
      ready_queue.enqueue(current_process)
    ELSE
      current_process.turnaround_time = current_time - current_process.arrival_time
      total_turnaround = total_turnaround + current_process.turnaround_time
    END IF
  END WHILE

  OUTPUT total_waiting/n, total_turnaround/n
END
examples.explanation: Round Robin gives each process equal CPU time slices, ensuring no starvation. Response time is good but throughput may suffer with small time quantum due to context switch overhead.

examples.title: Priority Scheduling with Aging
examples.description: Priority scheduling that implements aging to prevent starvation
examples.code:
def priority_scheduling_with_aging(processes):
    """
    Demonstrates priority scheduling with aging to prevent starvation.
    Aging increases priority of waiting processes over time.
    """
    current_time = 0
    completed = []
    ready_queue = processes.copy()

    # Initialize aging counter
    aging_factor = 1  # Priority increases by 1 every unit

    while len(completed) < len(processes):
        # Increase waiting process priorities (aging)
        for p in ready_queue:
            if p not in completed and p.arrival_time <= current_time:
                p.priority += aging_factor

        # Sort by priority (higher priority first)
        ready_queue.sort(key=lambda x: x.priority, reverse=True)

        # Select highest priority process
        current_process = None
        for p in ready_queue:
            if p.arrival_time <= current_time and p not in completed:
                current_process = p
                break

        if current_process:
            # Execute process
            current_process.waiting_time = current_time - current_process.arrival_time
            current_time += current_process.burst_time
            current_process.turnaround_time = current_time - current_process.arrival_time
            completed.append(current_process)
        else:
            # Idle CPU until next process arrives
            current_time = min(p.arrival_time for p in ready_queue if p not in completed)

    return completed

# Example usage would show how aging prevents starvation
# by gradually increasing priority of low-priority waiting processes
examples.explanation: Priority scheduling assigns higher priority to important processes. Without aging, low-priority processes may starve. Aging increases priority based on waiting time, ensuring all processes eventually get CPU time.

examples.title: Calculate Scheduling Metrics
examples.description: C program to calculate CPU scheduling criteria metrics
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates calculation of all scheduling metrics: waiting time, turnaround time, and response time. Shows the mathematical relationships between these criteria.
